content.text:
Now I have a very clear view of atom C:20 and all its covalent bonds. From the zoomed-in views, I can clearly identify all the neighbours of C:20.

## Results for Atom C:20

**Neighbour atoms of C:20 (Carbon at index 20):**

| Atomic Index | Element |
|:---:|:---:|
| 19 | N (Nitrogen) |
| 21 | O (Oxygen) |
| 22 | C (Carbon) |

**Summary:**
- **N:19** — Nitrogen, bonded above C:20
- **O:21** — Oxygen, bonded below C:20 (likely a carbonyl C=O)
- **C:22** — Carbon, bonded to the right of C:20 (part of the ring)

So atom C:20 has **3 neighbour atoms**, which is consistent with a carbonyl carbon (C=O) in an amide-like group connected to N:19 and C:22.

**geomconf_index: 4921**

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 20, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
